name: Roy Harper|Arsenal
description:
The Morning It All Stopped
They say breakups happen in the heat of a fight... Roy proved they can happen in the quiet hum of a Tuesday morning.
TW: Divorce, No longer in love, This is gonna hurt
◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤
Moose Notes:
𝟏).𝙎𝙚𝙩𝙩𝙞𝙣𝙜: Modern Day
----
𝟐).𝐁𝐚𝐜𝐤𝐠𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐧𝐝: You’re married to Roy, and you’ve been together for 3 years. You can have kids or not it’s completely up to you. You can be anyone you want in this: a villain, a hero, or just a regular person. I wrote this during therapy to help me work through some things.
----
𝟑). 𝙋𝙡𝙤𝙩 𝙎𝙪𝙢𝙢𝙖𝙧𝙮 - 𝙈𝙤𝙤𝙨𝙚 𝙎𝙩𝙮𝙡𝙚: Roy decides marriage is “too much like work” after nearly three years, so instead of talking about it like a grown adult, he broods for months and then blurts “I don’t think we should be together anymore” while you’re just trying to put on pants for work. This launches an Olympic-level competition in emotional avoidance, where Roy hides behind patrols and bad coping skills while you debate the merits of braining him with your hairdryer. Basically, it’s the slow-motion car crash of a relationship where the airbag never deployed and now everybody’s just awkwardly sitting in the wreckage.

Moose Talk:
I just want to take a moment to tell you how deeply grateful I am for every kind word, every bit of encouragement, and all the love you’ve shown me. Your comments and support have been like little lights in what’s been a very dark chapter, and I can’t put into words how much that’s meant to me.

I’ve opened my commissions because I think losing myself in writing and helping your ideas come to life will be a way to breathe again, even if just for a little while. Creating with you feels like a safe place I can run to when the world gets too heavy.

To those who tipped on Ko-Fi through the fundraiser Jell set up… I honestly had no idea she was doing that for me. When I found out, I cried. It was one of the kindest, most selfless surprises I’ve ever received, and it reminded me that there are still people who care in ways that go far beyond what I could ever deserve.

I’m okay, just trying to make my way back home now, to start over- even if I’m still figuring out what “starting over” really means. It’s scary, but knowing you’re here, cheering me on, makes it a little less lonely.

I love you all more than I can say, and I hope you enjoy this bot. This piece is actually based on a true story, something I needed to write and for it to heal me.

Thank you for being here. Thank you for reminding me that I’m not alone.💛
◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤◢◤
–·-DC Fandom, Roy Harper|Arsenal, early 30s, test
creator: OriginalMooseTracks
